mnemonics.title: Bias vs Variance
mnemonics.content: BIV: Bias means model is Blatantly Ignoring Validation data; Variance means model is Very Varied between datasets. Underfit = HIGH Bias, Overfit = HIGH Variance.

mnemonics.title: L1 vs L2 Regularization
mnemonics.content: L1 LASSO: Leaves Almost Some Strings of Output (sparsity/zero weights). L2 RIDGE: Really Impressive Dense Generalization (shrinks but doesn't zero).

mnemonics.title: Bias-Variance Tradeoff Signs
mnemonics.content: HIGH BIAS = High Train, High Test = 'Two HIGH' = Underfit. LOW Train, HIGH Test = 'One HIGH One LOW' = Overfit.

mnemonics.title: Regularization Techniques
mnemonics.content: DROP: Dropout, Regularize weights, Optimize validation, Prevent overfitting (Early stopping)

acronyms.expansion: Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator
acronyms.usage: L1 regularization that performs feature selection by setting some weights to zero.

acronyms.expansion: Regression with Individual Thresholding for Degree Estimation
acronyms.usage: L2 regularization that shrinks weights towards zero but keeps all features.
Combines L1 and L2 penalties for both feature selection and weight shrinkage.
Neural network architecture where overfitting is a common concern addressed by regularization.

visualTips.visualization: Think of a target/bullseye diagram: High bias shots are clustered away from center (consistently wrong), high variance shots are scattered widely (inconsistent).
Draw a U-curve: left side = underfitting zone (high bias), right side = overfitting zone (high variance), bottom = optimal complexity point.
Imagine weights as springs: L2 shrinks springs gently (damped), L1 cuts some springs completely (on/off), Dropout randomly breaks connections forcing redundancy.
Compare a straight line (high bias) vs a squiggly line passing through every point (high variance). Optimal is a smooth curve capturing true pattern.

analogies.analogy: A biased model is like a colorblind person trying to sort colored socks - they consistently make the same errors regardless of the specific socks.
Variance is like weather forecasting that changes dramatically with minor variations in data - one day predicting rain, next day sunshine for similar conditions.
Regularization is like a coach preventing an athlete from overtraining - it adds rest/constraints to improve long-term performance.
Dropout is like studying for exams by randomly covering parts of notes - forces you to truly understand rather than memorize specific arrangements.
L1 is like a budget that makes you choose only essential items (sparse), L2 is like a budget that just makes everything cheaper but you can still buy everything.
